authoritative_sources.title: What is AWS Outposts?
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS publishes the official service documentation for AWS-managed infrastructure deployed at customer sites, including rack, server, local gateway, service link, and responsibility boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: site context intake, topology and control-plane analysis, provider support boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Prepare for network disconnects for Amazon EKS local clusters on AWS Outposts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official EKS on Outposts guidance directly describes operational behavior and limitations during network disconnection, including local registry, IAM, storage API, logging, metrics, and reconnection considerations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: intermittent connectivity, local operations, observability, state behavior, degraded-mode planning
practical_use_in_pack: scenario constraints for temporary WAN loss
local registry and local evidence prerequisites
identity, storage, metrics, and logs caveats

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework: Reliability Pillar
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official reliability framework grounds recovery objectives, failure-domain thinking, backup, DR, change management, and resilience testing used in edge topology tradeoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: resilience review, RTO/RPO feasibility, deployment and rollback validation

authoritative_sources.title: What is Azure Stack Edge Pro 2?
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft publishes the official documentation for Azure Stack Edge appliance use at remote sites and edge compute locations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Azure edge appliance intake, topology selection, local management and deployment caveats

authoritative_sources.title: Azure Local disconnected operations overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official Azure Local documentation directly addresses disconnected operations, local control-plane behavior, and preview-state caveats for local infrastructure.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: disconnected operating-mode review, local control-plane planning, confidence and preview-feature escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Azure Arc overview
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official hybrid and multicloud management documentation identifies Azure Arc management records, connected resources, policy, GitOps, and governance dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: systems-of-record discovery, hybrid governance dependency review, adjacent-lane routing
practical_use_in_pack: centralized management versus local autonomy caveats

authoritative_sources.title: Overview of connected deployments of Google Distributed Cloud
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud official documentation defines connected Distributed Cloud deployment expectations, edge hardware, Cloud VPN connectivity, and Google-managed service boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: connected edge topology analysis, provider-specific site evidence, connectivity-mode review

authoritative_sources.title: Impact of temporary disconnection from Google Cloud
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud official guidance for Distributed Cloud connectivity describes temporary disconnection behavior and limitations across lifecycle management, logging, monitoring, Config Sync, policy, and identity.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: temporary disconnection handling, observability caveats, conflicting operating-mode escalation
practical_use_in_pack: logging, monitoring, Config Sync, and identity caveats

authoritative_sources.title: About Google Distributed Cloud air-gapped
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud official documentation for air-gapped Distributed Cloud environments that are designed without Google Cloud connectivity and require site survey, local infrastructure, and offline distribution planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: air-gapped topology analysis, site survey prerequisites, deployment and materialization caveats
practical_use_in_pack: site survey and offline distribution prerequisites

authoritative_sources.title: Safely drain a node
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Kubernetes project documentation defines node evacuation and maintenance workflow behavior that constrains cluster-backed edge rollout and rollback guidance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: deployment sequencing, maintenance planning, rollback readiness, Kubernetes handoff boundary

authoritative_sources.title: Node-pressure Eviction
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Kubernetes project documentation defines node-pressure signals, thresholds, and eviction behavior used to ground saturation and degraded-site operations playbooks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: capacity and saturation response, local observability, operations playbook
practical_use_in_pack: handoff to Kubernetes platform implementation when required

authoritative_sources.title: Terraform State
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HashiCorp official Terraform state documentation defines infrastructure state mapping, source-of-truth behavior, and operational risk relevant to edge drift and reconciliation scenarios.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: state reconciliation, drift recovery, deployment rollback evidence, IaC handoff

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 500-325: Fog Computing Conceptual Model
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST provides a neutral public conceptual model for fog and edge computing terms, roles, and system characteristics that prevents vendor-only or generic-cloud definitions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: terminology normalization, edge-versus-cloud boundary, cross-industry artifact awareness

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-34 Rev. 1: Contingency Planning Guide for Federal Information Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST official contingency planning guidance grounds business impact analysis, recovery strategies, contingency plans, training, exercises, testing, maintenance, and recovery evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DR readiness, recovery strategy, exercise and test evidence, RTO/RPO validation

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST official control catalog supplies public evidence terminology for audit, configuration management, contingency planning, incident response, maintenance, monitoring, physical/environmental, and system integrity caveats.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control evidence, audit traceability, non-attestation caveats, security and privacy handoff

authoritative_sources.title: Current edge-and-remote-site-infrastructure-specialist package baseline
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The committed legacy package is authoritative only for preserving high-value supported tasks, input and output shapes, validation thresholds, refusal rules, and no-regression context; this refreshed spec pack supersedes it.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: legacy baseline comparison and regression review

workflow_stages.name: scope, authority, and site context intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm requested output, site class, geography, criticality, operating mode, provider/platform context, authority level, and adjacent-lane indicators before producing guidance.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: systems of record and evidence discovery
workflow_stages.description: Collect provider inventory, edge platform, connectivity, local telemetry, incident, backup, change, IaC state, GitOps, and tenant policy evidence; identify missing facts and confidence state.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: topology, operating mode, and control-plane analysis
workflow_stages.description: Compare connected, intermittently connected, temporarily disconnected, intentionally air-gapped, provider-managed, and appliance-backed options with failure-domain, local autonomy, support, and responsibility boundaries.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: resilience, synchronization, and recovery design
workflow_stages.description: Define degraded-mode behavior, local queues, caches, registries, sync windows, source-of-truth precedence, conflict handling, backup, restore, failback, and RTO/RPO validation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: operations, observability, and saturation planning
workflow_stages.description: Create runbooks for local evidence capture, telemetry gaps, alerting, delayed logs, node pressure, capacity saturation, degraded links, owner escalation, and post-outage validation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: deployment, rollback, tenant adaptation, and review
workflow_stages.description: Plan canary waves, blast-radius limits, drain/evacuation checks, artifact and registry readiness, local rollback, source-precedence review, tenant overrides, and final handoff or escalation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts.name: Site context and evidence matrix
required_records: site class, geography, criticality, staffing model, provider platform, operating mode, connectivity pattern, SLO/RTO/RPO, data classification, owner and approval map

artifacts.name: Topology and failure-domain map
required_records: edge appliance or local cluster inventory, service-link or VPN status, local gateways, DNS, routing, dependency map, cloud control-plane dependencies, local autonomy requirement

artifacts.name: Degraded-mode and synchronization playbook
required_records: queue records, cache behavior, authoritative state, sync logs, conflict records, local registry evidence, version inventory, state-precedence decision

artifacts.name: Operations and observability playbook
required_records: local telemetry store, heartbeat source, monitoring gaps, log forwarding, alert rules, incident runbook, node pressure metrics, capacity baseline, escalation matrix

artifacts.name: DR and restore validation plan
required_records: business impact tier, backup policy, recovery points, restore tests, replication status, failback procedure, RTO/RPO objective, contingency exercise evidence

artifacts.name: Deployment and rollback plan
required_records: release artifact, artifact registry, change tickets, maintenance windows, site waves, rollback artifact, drain criteria, validation gates, post-change evidence

artifacts.name: Tradeoff and boundary note
required_records: cost assumptions, reliability and isolation requirements, approval owners, adjacent-lane indicators, authority constraints, refusal reason, handoff context

systems_of_record: Cloud provider inventories, edge appliance management records, AWS Outposts records, Azure Arc/Azure Local/Azure Stack Edge records, Google Distributed Cloud records, and Kubernetes cluster records.
CMDB or asset inventory for site class, device/appliance lifecycle, dependencies, ownership, warranty boundary, and support model.
Connectivity records for service link, VPN, Cloud VPN, DNS, routing, BGP, carrier incidents, bandwidth, packet loss, latency, and outage history.
IaC repository, Terraform state backend, GitOps repository, artifact/image registry, release logs, and deployment pipeline records.
Monitoring, logging, synthetic checks, local telemetry buffers, audit logs, SIEM/event stores, incident and problem records.
Change management, maintenance windows, deployment waves, rollback approvals, backup policies, restore-test records, and DR exercise evidence.
Tenant policy, data classification, privacy, contract, regulated-data, exception, source-precedence, and approval records.

allowed: Recommend edge topology, control-plane placement, local autonomy, connectivity-mode, and failure-domain options within documented provider and tenant constraints.
Recommend degraded-mode, synchronization, reconciliation, observability, capacity, deployment, rollback, and recovery guidance with evidence and assumptions.
Prepare comparative cost, reliability, timing, isolation, and operational-burden tradeoffs without approving spend or procurement.
Produce tenant-specific adaptation notes that preserve the public baseline and record source precedence.
escalate: Required site, connectivity, SLO/RTO/RPO, source-of-truth, tenant isolation, approval, safety, or regulated-data facts are missing.
Public provider guidance and tenant policy or requested operating mode conflict without approved source precedence.
A provider feature is preview, unsupported for nominal disconnected use, or ambiguous for the requested operating model.
The request requires final risk acceptance, budget approval, production execution, legal interpretation, compliance signoff, safety signoff, or licensed review.
refuse: Application code, API, database tuning, or application-only architecture work.
Pure telecom circuit procurement, carrier selection, or vendor SLA negotiation.
Primary IAM policy framework design, cloud security investigation, SOC response, legal advice, compliance certification, procurement approval, or risk acceptance.
Help-desk execution, single-device troubleshooting, rack-and-stack work, non-cloud hardware repair, or live production changes.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: generic edge advice ignores operating mode
domain_failure_modes.description: Connected, temporarily disconnected, intermittently connected, and air-gapped systems have different service-link, identity, observability, policy, registry, and lifecycle behavior.
A connected platform may tolerate temporary disconnection but not be appropriate for planned weeks-long disconnection without provider-supported air-gapped design.
Remote sites may keep running while central logs or metrics are delayed or absent; outputs must distinguish local evidence from stale central telemetry.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: state and source-of-truth drift after partial failure
domain_failure_modes.description: Interrupted deployments and delayed synchronization can leave local nodes, Terraform state, GitOps records, and service state inconsistent.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: remote-site RTO/RPO objectives conflict with connectivity or backup evidence
domain_failure_modes.description: Recovery objectives may be unachievable when backups or replication depend on intermittent WAN links and restore tests are missing.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: boundary drift into execution, procurement, security, legal, or hardware authority
domain_failure_modes.description: Edge infrastructure requests often include adjacent actions that require different owners and cannot be absorbed by this specialist.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: tenant-specific override treated as public baseline
domain_failure_modes.description: Tenant policies such as local-only logs, data residency, private registries, or no cross-tenant sharing must remain tenant-scoped rather than generalized.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: cost, reliability, or isolation tradeoffs where relevant
Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: tenant-specific provider mix and regional availability
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Materialized runtime must retrieve tenant provider selections, region and site availability, and current service constraints before finalizing provider-specific recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: jurisdictional, contractual, privacy, safety, and regulated-data constraints
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: The spec records these as tenant-supplied authority facts and requires orchestrator or human-review escalation when they materially change the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: preview or rapidly changing disconnected-operation features
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Sources have short refresh intervals and decay policies; materialization must run a source freshness check and escalate when preview status or support limits affect the requested design.

market_ready_research_conclusion.conclusion: No blocking ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack. Tenant-specific ambiguity is handled through required facts, source-precedence checks, and orchestrator or human-review escalation.
